Grant Chapman
Electrical & Embedded Systems Engineering

I design PCBs and write firmware for energy systems.

Third-year Electrical Engineering student at the University of Waterloo. I build battery management tools, power converters, and CAN bus diagnostics — from schematic to field-tested hardware.

Resume GitHub LinkedIn
C/C++ Altium CAN Bus ESP32 Python KiCad LTSpice PlatformIO SystemVerilog SPI I2C UART USB-C Modbus WiFi/HTTP Git PCAN-View MATLAB VBA Oscilloscopes Logic Analyzers
Portfolio

Projects

CAN Bus Diagnostic Tool

4-layer PCB integrating ESP32-S3, MCP2515 SPI-CAN controller, dual SN65HVD230 transceivers, and USB-C serial. Decodes 250/500 kbps CAN streams from 24-cell battery packs and serves real-time telemetry over WiFi. Dual-source power architecture with TPS54302, BQ24075, and TPS63021 for untethered field operation. Built at Generac.

Altium C++ ESP32-S3 CAN Bus SPI Power Mgmt
View on GitHub →

12V-to-5V Synchronous Buck Converter

Designed in LTSpice and laid out in Altium with EMI suppression techniques for the Midnight Sun solar race vehicle. Powers control lights from the 12V bus. Integrated into the car's electrical system alongside CAN telemetry between motor controllers and BMS.

Altium LTSpice Power Electronics EMI/EMC

All Digital PLL

ADPLL with Bang-Bang phase detector in SystemVerilog for a 108µm × 20µm tile. Implemented gear shifting in the loop filter and verified lock robustness via CocoTB with automated leaky bucket testing.

SystemVerilog Python OpenLane CocoTB
View on GitHub →

Work

Experience

Generac
Cambridge, ON
Jan – Apr 2026

Battery Systems Engineering Intern

  • Developed C++ firmware using ESP32-S3 TWAI and MCP2515 SPI-CAN controller to decode 250/500 kbps CAN streams from 24-cell battery packs, reducing system validation time by 75% via WiFi-hosted embedded HTTP server
  • Designed and routed a 4-layer PCB in Altium integrating ESP32-S3, MCP2515, dual SN65HVD230 transceivers, and USB-C serial — adopted by the team for field diagnostics
  • Reverse-engineered proprietary extended CAN IDs from raw bus traces using PCAN-View, applying bitwise masking for real-time cell voltage, pack voltage, and temperature telemetry
  • Engineered dual-source power architecture (TPS54302 + BQ24075 + TPS63021) enabling untethered wireless operation during BESS field testing
Midnight Sun Solar Rayce Car
Waterloo, ON
Nov 2025 – Present

Battery Systems Team Member

  • Designed a 12V-to-5V synchronous buck converter in LTSpice and laid out PCB in Altium with EMI suppression — integrated into race vehicle for control light power delivery
  • Integrated CAN bus telemetry between motor controllers and BMS for real-time performance logging and fault detection during endurance racing
  • Designed power distribution units (PDU) optimizing thermal management and EMC in a weight-critical chassis
